---
title: "X_DataTables"
output: html_document
---

########################################## 
#                                        #
#          GMA Project - Tables          #
#                                        #
########################################## 


```{r}
setwd("~/Workspace/GMA_Scripts/")
load("MeansTable.RData")
source("./0_Formulas.R")

#packages
library(clipr)
library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)
```

### Generate LaTeX table of variance components
```{r}
#Reshape data to wide format
variance_fmtd <- variance |>
  # select(!c(sig2F1,sig2P2,sig2P1,sig2F2)) |>
  mutate(
    Dataset = factor(
      Dataset,
      levels = levels(variance$Dataset),
      labels = c("Raw", "BLUEs")
    )
  ) |>
  left_join(
    traits |> select(trait, name),
    by = c("trait")
  ) |>
  select(!trait) |>
  relocate(name, .before = everything()) |>
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.numeric),
      ~ ifelse(.x == 0, paste0("0.0", "\\textsuperscript{*}"), as.character(.x))
    )
  )

# Create LaTeX-friendly column names
colnames(variance_fmtd) <- c(
  "Trait",
  "Dataset",
  "$\\hat \\sigma^2_{env}$",
  "$\\hat \\sigma^2_{gF_{2}}$",
  "$\\hat \\sigma^2_{a}$",
  "$\\hat \\sigma^2_{d}$",
  "$\\hat h^2_{b}$",
  "$\\hat h^2_{n}$"
)

latex_table <- kable(
  variance_fmtd,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  caption = "Variance components and heritability estimates across original and predicted datasets. Values represent variance components ($\\sigma^{2}$) and heritability estimates ($h^{2}$).",
  label = "tab:variance_components",
  row.names = FALSE,
  escape = FALSE
) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "middle") |>
  footnote(
    symbol = c("Negative variances and heritabilities were assigned a value of zero.")
  ) |>
  kable_styling(position = "center")

# Print the LaTeX code of variance components
clipr::write_clip(latex_table)
```

### Generate LaTeX table of AIC and BICs
```{r}
tbl <- c(
  "Trait" = "name",
  "$F - Corr$" = "wospcor",
  "$F - Irr$" = "woirr",
  "Full model" = "full",
  "$\\Delta_{_{F\\to F-i}$" = "difirr",
  "$\\Delta\\%_{_{F\\to F-i}}$" = "perirr",
  "$\\Delta_{_{F\\to F-c}}$" = "difspcor",
  "$\\Delta\\%_{_{F\\to F-c}}$" = "perspcor"
)

xic_df <- ics_df$bic |>
  left_join(
    traits |> select(abrv, name),
    by = c("value_id" = "abrv")
  ) |>
  relocate(c(name, full, woirr), .before = value_id) |>
  select(!value_id) |>
  rename(all_of(tbl)) |>
  glimpse()

xic_tex <- kable(
  xic_df,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  caption = "Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC) scores for model comparison across evaluated traits. Three models were compared: (1) Full model (Eq.~\\ref{glmm}) including all parameters; (2) $F - Irr$: Full model excluding the irrigation parameter ($\\beta_{1}I_{k}$); and (3) $F - Corr$: Full model excluding autoregressive residual correlation (assuming {\\small $\\varepsilon_{ijkl} \\sim N(0, \\sigma^2_{\\varepsilon} \\boldsymbol{I})$}). Delta values ($\\Delta$) represent the difference in AIC scores between the Full and reduced models, with $\\Delta\\%$ indicating the percentage change. Positve $\\Delta$ values indicate improved model fit when parameters are excluded.",
  label = "bic",
  row.names = FALSE,
  escape = FALSE,
  digits = 1
)

clipr::write_clip(xic_tex)
```

### Generate LaTeX table of GMA additive+dominance and epistatic model
```{r}
latex_table <- list()

traitsEPI <- traits |>
  filter(trait %in% c(
    "num_sideshoot",
    "total_leaf_sideshoot",
    "sideshoot_length"
  ))

for (i in 1:nrow(traitsEPI)){

  gma_fmtd <- gma_list[[traitsEPI$abrv[i]]]

  gma_fmtd <- gma_fmtd  |>
    select(!abrv) |>
    mutate(
      Dataset = factor(
        Dataset,
        levels = levels(gma_fmtd$Dataset),
        labels = c("Raw", "BLUEs")
      )
    ) |>
    mutate(
      model = factor(
        model,
        levels = levels(gma_fmtd$model),
        labels = c(
          paste0(
            "A+D ($",
            sprintf("%.3f", as.numeric(levels(gma_fmtd$model)[1])),
            "$)\\textsuperscript{1}"
          ),
          "Epistatic",
          paste0(
            "A+D ($",
            sprintf("%.3f", as.numeric(levels(gma_fmtd$model)[3])),
            "$)\\textsuperscript{1}"
          )
        )
      )
    ) |>
    mutate(
      parameters = factor(
        parameters,
        levels = levels(gma_fmtd$parameters),
        labels = c(
          "$\\hat{a}$",
          "$\\hat{d}$",
          "$\\hat{m}$",
          "$\\widehat{aa}$",
          "$\\widehat{ad}$",
          "$\\widehat{dd}$"
        )
      )
    )

  colnames(gma_fmtd) <- c(
    "Dataset",
    "Model ($r^{2}$)",
    "$\\hat{\\beta}_{i}$",
    "Estimates",
    "$\\hat{\\sigma}^2_{\\beta_{i}}$",
    "$\\hat{\\sigma}_{\\beta_{i}}$",
    "$t$-test",
    "$df$",
    "$p$-value",
    "Sum of squares",
    "$R^{2}$(\\%)"
  )

  # Generate LaTeX table with booktabs formatting
  latex_table[[i]] <- kable(
    gma_fmtd,
    align = "lllcccccccc",
    valign = "[t]",
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    table.envir = "sidewaystable",
    caption = paste0(
      "Parameters ($\\hat{\\beta}_{i}$) estimates for ",
      tolower(traitsEPI$name[i]),
      " under additive-dominance (A+D) model and epistatic (A+D+I) model using raw and BLUEs datasets. ",
      "Values include estimate variances ($\\hat{\\sigma}^2_{\\beta_{i}}$), ",
      "standard errors ($\\hat{\\sigma}_{\\beta_{i}}$), ",
      "significance levels, degrees of freedom ($df$), sum of squares, ",
      "and relative contributions ($R^{2}$) of each parameter."
    ),
    label = paste0(i + 1, "gma:", traitsEPI$trait[i]),
    row.names = FALSE,
    escape = FALSE
  ) |>
    collapse_rows(columns = c(1, 2), valign = "middle") |>
    footnote(
      number = c("Squared correlation coefficient (r²) between observed generation means and calculated means from the A+D model.")
    ) |>
    kable_styling(position = "center")

}
out <- unlist(latex_table)
# Print the LaTeX code of GMA tables for additive+dominance and epistatic models
clipr::write_clip(out)
```


### Generate LaTeX table of GMA additive+dominance model
```{r}
latex_table <- list()

traitsEPI <- traits |> filter(trait %in% c(
  "num_sideshoot",
  "total_leaf_sideshoot",
  "sideshoot_length"
))

traitsAD <- setdiff(traits, traitsEPI)

for (i in 1:nrow(traitsAD)){

  gma_fmtd <- gma_list[[traitsAD$abrv[i]]]

  gma_fmtd <- gma_fmtd  |>
    select(!abrv) |>
    filter(model != "epi") |>
    droplevels()
  gma_fmtd <- gma_fmtd  |>
    mutate(
      Dataset = factor(
        Dataset,
        levels = levels(gma_fmtd$Dataset),
        labels = c("Raw", "BLUEs")
      )
    ) |>
    mutate(
      model = factor(
        model,
        levels = levels(gma_fmtd$model),
        labels = c(
          paste0("A+D ($", levels(gma_fmtd$model)[1], "$)\\textsuperscript{1}"),
          paste0("A+D ($", levels(gma_fmtd$model)[2], "$)\\textsuperscript{1}")
        )
      )
    ) |>
    mutate(
      parameters = factor(
        parameters,
        levels = levels(gma_fmtd$parameters),
        labels = c("$\\hat{a}$",
          "$\\hat{d}$",
          "$\\hat{m}$"
        )
      )
    ) |>
    mutate(pval = round(pval, 4))

  colnames(gma_fmtd) <- c(
    "Dataset",
    "Model ($r^{2}$)",
    "$\\hat{\\beta_{i}}$",
    "Estimates",
    "$\\hat \\sigma^2_{\\beta_{i}}$",
    "$\\hat \\sigma_{\\beta_{i}}$",
    "$t$-test",
    "$df$",
    "$p$-value",
    "Sum of squares",
    "$R^{2}$(\\%)"
  )

  # Generate LaTeX table with booktabs formatting
  latex_table[[i]] <- kable(
    gma_fmtd,
    align = "lllcccccccc",
    valign = "[t]",
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    # table.envir = "sidewaystable",
    caption = paste0(
      "Parameters ($\\hat{\\beta_{i}}$) estimates for ",
      tolower(traitsAD$name[i]),
      " under additive-dominance (A+D) model using raw and BLUEs datasets. ",
      "Values include estimate variances ($\\hat{\\sigma}^2_{\\beta_{i}}$), ",
      "standard errors ($\\hat{\\sigma}_{\\beta_{i}}$), ",
      "significance levels, degrees of freedom ($df$), sum of squares, ",
      "and relative contributions ($R^{2}$) of each parameter."
    ),
    label = paste0(i + 1, "gma:", traitsAD$trait[i]),
    row.names = FALSE,
    escape = FALSE
  ) |> 
    collapse_rows(columns = c(1, 2), valign = "middle") |>
    footnote(
      number = c("Squared correlation coefficient (r²) between observed generation means and calculated means from the A+D model.")
    ) |>
    kable_styling(position = "center")

}

out <- unlist(latex_table)

# Print the LaTeX code of GMA tables for additive+dominance and epistatic models
clipr::write_clip(out)
```

##Figures labels
#Variograms (individually)
```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}
vario <- paste0("\\begin{figure}[htbp]
  \\centering
  \\includegraphics[width=1.0\\textwidth, angle=-90]{figs/vario_", traits$trait, ".eps}
  \\caption{Variogram of ", tolower(traits$name), " using the autoregressive correlation residual structure and irrigation spatial covariate.}\\label{fig:vario_", traits$trait, "}
\\end{figure}
")
clipr::write_clip(vario)
```

#Variograms 2x6 block
```{r}
lista <- list()
traitsARV <- traits |> filter(trait %in% c(
  "height_to_det",
  "total_leaf_sideshoot",
  "hypocotyl_diam",
  "num_clusters_to_det",
  "internode_length",
  "lai"
))

n <- 1
# n <- n + 6

traitsARV$print <- paste0("    \\begin{subfigure}{0.45\\textwidth}
       \\centering
       \\includegraphics[width=\\linewidth, angle=-90]{figs/vario_", traitsARV$trait,".eps}
       \\caption{", traitsARV$name, "}
    \\end{subfigure}")

lista[["beg"]] <- "\\begin{figure}[htbp]
  \\centering\n"

for (j in (n):(n + 5)){
  lista[[paste0(j, 1)]] <- traitsARV$print[j]
  if (j %% 2 == 1) {
    lista[[paste0(j, 2)]] <- "    \\hfill"
  } else {
    if (j == (n + 5)) {

    } else {
      lista[[paste0(j, 2)]] <- "\n"
    }
  }
}

lista[["end"]] <- "  \\caption{Variograms of trait measurements using an autoregressive residual correlation structure and irrigation spatial covariate, showing how variance changes with distance. The $z$-axis represents the variance across different distances (lag) based on row (line) and plant position (plant n°).}
  \\label{fig:vario_onetosix}
\\end{figure}"


clipr::write_clip(paste0(lista))
cat(paste0(lista))
```

#Ruler 3x4 block
```{r}
traitsEPI <- traits |> filter(trait %in% c(
  "num_sideshoot",
  "total_leaf_sideshoot",
  "sideshoot_length"
))

traitsAD <- setdiff(traits, traitsEPI)

traitsA <- traitsAD |> filter(abrv %in% c("htd", "lai"))
traitsPD <- traitsAD |> filter(abrv %in% c("nltfc", "llag", "inl", "nctd"))
traitsD <- traitsAD |> filter(abrv %in% c("nlafc", "la"))
traitsOD <- traitsAD |> filter(abrv %in% c("nll", "hyd", "la", "nctd", "tcss", "lag"))

lista <- list()

traitsX <- traitsOD

n <- 1

traitsX$print <- paste0("    \\begin{subfigure}{0.35\\textwidth}
       \\centering
       \\includegraphics[width=\\linewidth, angle=-90]{figs/ruler_", traitsX$abrv,".eps}
       \\caption{",traitsX$name,"}
    \\end{subfigure}")

lista[["beg"]] <- "\\begin{figure}[htbp]
  \\centering\n"

for (j in (n):nrow(traitsX)){
  lista[[paste0(j, 1)]] <- traitsX$print[j]
  if (j %% 3 == 1) {
    lista[[paste0(j, 3)]] <- "    \\hfill"
  } else {
    if (j == (n + 11)) {

    } else {
      lista[[paste0(j, 3)]] <- "\n"
    }
  }
}

lista[["end"]] <- "  \\caption{Representation of the additive-dominance model for traits with absence of dominance. $AA$ is the dominant homozygote, $Aa$ is the heterozygote, $aa$ is the recessive homozygote, $a$ is the additive effect, $d$ is the dominance effect, $m$ is the midpoint between homozygotes, and $\\mu$ is the mean value.}
  \\label{fig:ruler}
\\end{figure}"


clipr::write_clip(paste0(lista))
```
